notes: Pass rate ties at 100% both configs - the assertions do not discriminate: the model is already competent with herdr's discoverable CLI (--help + JSON output). Skill value shows in speed, tokens, and safety behavior instead.

Time per eval (with vs without): session-lifecycle 188s vs 358s (48% faster with skill); pane-grid 213s vs 190s (baseline marginally faster); window-management 296s vs 386s (23% faster). Mean delta -79s, -7k tokens.

SAFETY (not captured by assertions): the baseline window-management run attached to and stopped the DEFAULT session server with no live-agent check - on a machine with live agents that kills them all. The with-skill run ran herdr-cleanup.ps1 -DryRun (live-agent inventory) before stopping anything.
Baseline knowledge win folded back into the skill: HERDR_SESSION env var (simpler session isolation than HERDR_SOCKET_PATH) is real (src/session.rs:10) and is now documented in SKILL.md.

Two script defects surfaced by the with-skill window run and fixed post-run: FindWindowW exact-title false-fails from elevated shells (now falls back to WindowsTerminal MainWindowTitle match), and wt --maximized is unreliable (now enforced via ShowWindow SW_MAXIMIZE). Iteration-1 results reflect the pre-fix script.
Iteration-2 candidates: a discriminating orchestration eval (msg bus routing + team spawn with a scripted fake agent), and re-run of window-management against the fixed script.
